rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on copycat/private-label similarity, trademark/trade-dress context, and consumer evaluation outcomes, and the cited paper directly studies when high vs. moderate similarity helps or hurts copycats. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution against prior imitation/confusion-focused work, making this citation plausibly part of its core theoretical background rather than an incidental mention. With an exact DOI match (10.1509/jmr.08.0405), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
